method_atom: a one-parameter deformation of standard exponential statistics, derived from a generalized (deformed) entropy functional, that replaces the ordinary exponential distribution with a deformed power-law distribution controlled by a single deformation parameter. The parameter is fixed by the external driving gradients acting on the system, and the resulting stationary distribution characterizes a meta-equilibrium state rather than true thermodynamic equilibrium.
object_atom: a relativistic gas of particles immersed in an external electromagnetic field, described by its temperature four-gradient and the gradient of the four-vector potential.
brief_reason: the deformed-entropy/deformation-parameter machinery yielding power-law meta-equilibrium distributions is the transferable method, while the relativistic gas in an electromagnetic field (four-gradients of temperature and potential) is the physics-specific object.
